whatRequiresPermit: New construction, alteration, repair, demolition, or relocation of commercial structures
Structural framing, additions, and new builds
Electrical, plumbing, gas, mechanical, and HVAC
Fire safety systems, elevators, signage, and asbestos abatement
uniqueConsiderations: Trade permits are SEPARATE for each trade: electrical, plumbing, gas, HVAC/mechanical, fire systems — each must be submitted by a licensed contractor in that field
Strict asbestos abatement requirements — overseen by local building departments AND MassDEP
780 CMR is localized — each city/town interprets and applies it differently
Inspections must be scheduled within 48 hours of request (per 780 CMR), but may take longer in practice due to staffing
Energy code + Stretch code — Massachusetts enforces IECC but many cities also enforce a Stretch Energy Code exceeding base requirements

applicationRequirements.item: Completed application form including project valuation
Sealed architectural/structural plans from Massachusetts-licensed professionals
Trade permit checklists (electrical, gas, plumbing, mechanical, or fire safety)
Asbestos abatement documentation for renovation or demolition work
Site plan (for new buildings, relocations, or fire systems)
Insurance certificates
Contractor licenses
Property ownership proof

processSteps: Pre-submittal meeting for large-scale projects (scope, trades, asbestos, stakeholders)
Submit application packet (forms, sealed plans, trade checklists, asbestos report, insurance, fees)
Plan review period — municipality issues corrections (typical 30-day window)
Pay issuance fees and pick up permit (post on-site)
Schedule inspections (footing, framing, trades, fire protection, final)
Certificate of Occupancy issuance after final inspection

fees.notes: Additional fees may apply for inspections, plan reviews, or zoning approvals

applicationRequirements.item: Project plans — detailed drawings or blueprints
Estimated construction cost
Property information (address, owner info, authorization to work)
Contractor information (names and license numbers of all contractors)
Zoning information (if changing use or building near property lines)

processSteps: Register at Boston ISD portal — determine which permits you need
Prepare application with detailed project plans
Submit application online and pay fees
Wait for ISD review (allow a few weeks)
Check status via Boston Permit Finder
Once approved: schedule inspections at various stages
Complete project, pass all inspections, close the permit

requirements.item: Article 85 Demolition Delay approval from Boston Landmarks Commission
requirements.sourceText: You must obtain an Article 85 Demolition Delay approval from the Boston Landmarks Commission.
requirements.reasoning: Required to verify building has no historic preservation status before demolition.

requirements.item: Utility shut-off notices (gas, electrical, telephone, cable)
requirements.sourceText: Shut off notices from all underground and overhead utilities, such as: gas, electrical, telephone and cable companies.
requirements.reasoning: All utilities must be disconnected before demolition can begin safely.

requirements.item: Boston Water and Sewer Commission (BWSC) closed GSA
requirements.sourceText: The Boston Water and Sewer Commission closed general service agreement GSA.
requirements.reasoning: Water and sewer connections must be formally closed and documented.

requirements.sourceText: Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) approval for hazardous materials 617-292-5500
requirements.reasoning: Massachusetts DEP must approve hazardous material handling and disposal plan.

requirements.sourceText: Dig Safe reference number is required.
requirements.reasoning: State law requires Dig Safe clearance before any ground disturbance.

requirements.sourceText: A letter or bill from a pest control contractor stating the pest management will be performed before and after the demolition project begins.
requirements.reasoning: Boston ISD requires documented pest management before and after demolition.

requirements.item: Licensed builder + Workers Comp, Mattocks Higgins Affidavit, contract, liability insurance, builder's license
requirements.sourceText: A licensed builder is required with supporting documents, such as: the Workers' Compensation Form, Mattocks Higgins Affidavit, contract between parties, liability insurance, copy of your builder's license front and back side.
requirements.reasoning: Boston ISD requires licensed contractor with full documentation package.

requirements.sourceText: Obtain a permit from Boston Fire Prevention pertaining to the demolition.
requirements.reasoning: Separate Fire Prevention permit required in addition to ISD demolition permit.

requirements.sourceText: You will also need a review from our Environmental Services Division on the 4th floor here at 1010 Massachusetts Avenue, or isdenvironmental@boston.gov
requirements.reasoning: ISD Environmental Services must review the demolition plan for environmental compliance.

whatRequiresPermit: Construct, alter, remove, repair, move, or demolish a building or structure
Change the use or occupancy of a structure
Abate or disturb asbestos, and related demolition or reconstruction
additionalApprovalsRequired: Historical Commission approval for work in historic/conservation districts or designated landmarks
Public Works or Transportation approvals for projects affecting curb cuts, sidewalks, or street access
whatDoesNotRequirePermit: Detached accessory structures (sheds) under 120 sq ft
Retaining walls up to 4 ft high

fees.certificateOfOccupancy: $100 for first unit + $50 per additional unit; $100 + $50 per 1,000 sq ft

applicationRequirements.item: Cambridge building permit application (via OpenGov portal)
Drawings/plans (PDF format)
Construction cost estimate
Contractor and subcontractor licenses
Energy compliance forms (for new construction/renovation)
Letter of Approval from Condominium Association (if applicable)
Photographs of existing conditions (e.g., exterior decks, exterior stairs)

whatRequiresPermit: Constructing, reconstructing, altering, repairing, removing, or demolishing a building or structure
Changing the use or occupancy of a building or structure
Installing or modifying equipment regulated by the Massachusetts State Building Code (780 CMR)

fees.tradeFees: All trade permits (electrical, plumbing, gas, mechanical): $20 per $1,000 ($50 minimum)

applicationRequirements.item: Site or architectural plans (PDF format)
Scope of work and valuation estimate
CSL (Construction Supervisor License) and/or HIC (Home Improvement Contractor Registration)
Energy code compliance documentation (REScheck or COMcheck)

salem.name: Salem
specialConsiderations: Numerous historic districts — many properties under historic preservation rules affecting what changes can be made
Coastal location adds complexity to certain project types

whatRequiresPermit: Structural work (cutting/altering load-bearing elements; changes affecting required egress)
Decks that are attached, or over 200 sq ft, or over 30 in above grade, or serving required exit door
Retaining walls over 4 ft high or supporting a surcharge
Roofing/siding/window/door replacements altering the building envelope
New construction, reconstruction, alteration, repair, removal, or demolition of a structure
whatDoesNotRequirePermit: Driveways (need separate driveway permit from City Engineer's Office instead)
Small storage sheds (≤120 sq ft and ≤16 ft tall, within setbacks, at least 10 ft from dwelling)

whatRequiresPermit: Demolition or moving any building or structure
Install, remove, or alter regulated systems (electrical, plumbing, mechanical, HVAC, gas, signs, fire protection)
whatDoesNotRequirePermit: Detached accessory structures (sheds) under 120 sq ft

electrical.electricalWithoutBuildingPermit: $50 minimum + $10/room (residential); $110 first floor + $50 each additional story (commercial)

whatRequiresPermit: Construct, reconstruct, alter, repair, or demolish a structure
Interior gutting (building permit, NOT demolition permit)
somervilleSpecificRules: Interior gutting = building permit. Total teardown = demolition permit.
3-family or larger: licensed contractor required to apply
Electrical: licensed electrician must apply for all electrical permits
Plumbing/gas: licensed plumber must apply for all permits
1-2 family owner-occupied: homeowner may obtain permit

licensedContractor.item: HIC (Home Improvement Contractor Registration) — if property is 1-4 units, owner-occupied

brockton.name: Brockton
brockton.notes: Follows 780 CMR. Contact local building department for specifics.

newBedford.name: New Bedford
newBedford.notes: Follows 780 CMR. Contact local building department for specifics.

zoningBoardOfAppeal.description: The ZBA hears appeals when a project is denied because it doesn't meet Boston's zoning code. Also handles variances and special permits.
zoningBoardOfAppeal.hearingLocation: 1 City Hall Square, Room 801, Boston MA 02201

process.name: File Appeal
process.detail: File within 45 days of denial letter. Online or in person at 1010 Mass Ave 5th Floor.

process.name: Pay Fee
process.detail: Residential ≤3 units: $150 flat. All other buildings or residential >3 units: $150 per City or state violation.

process.name: Hearing Set
process.detail: ZBA sets hearing date. Published at least 20 days ahead in Boston Globe and Herald. Adjacent owners notified.

process.name: Public Hearing
process.detail: Attend in person at City Hall Room 801 with supporting documents. Attorney/architect/professional allowed.

process.name: Decision
process.detail: Formal decision sent ~15 days after hearing. BRA may also need to review. Additional conditions may apply.

keyRules: Must file within 45 days of denial
If denied: must wait 1 year before re-appeal (unless major plan changes or dismissed without prejudice)
Cannot withdraw after notices are sent (can request dismissal without prejudice before board review)

article80.description: Article 80 of Boston's Zoning Code governs development review for large and small projects. Adopted 1996, most significant modernization voted July 2025.

largeProjectReview.threshold: Projects adding ≥50,000 sq ft gross floor area; projects near Boston Harbor; large projects with significant change of use without new construction
reviewAreas: Environmental protection (wind, shadow, noise, air quality, solar glare)

processSteps.name: Letter of Intent (LOI)
processSteps.detail: Developer files LOI describing the project with Boston Planning Dept.

processSteps.name: Project Notification Form (PNF)
processSteps.detail: Developer files PNF with further project description. Open for public comment.

processSteps.name: Draft Project Impact Report (DPIR)
processSteps.detail: If required: additional analysis of project impacts. Open for public comment.

processSteps.name: Final Project Impact Report (FPIR)
processSteps.detail: If required: addresses concerns from DPIR. Open for public comment.

processSteps.name: Adequacy Determination
processSteps.detail: BPDA Board votes to approve, not approve, or approve with conditions.

smallProjectReview.threshold: Projects adding ≥20,000 sq ft gross floor area OR ≥15 dwelling units (but less than 50,000 sq ft)
smallProjectReview.note: Notified by ISD or Planning Dept project manager if required

article80.recentUpdates: Most substantive set of changes to Article 80 since 1996 voted July 2025. Aimed at modernizing and making process more predictable and transparent.

demolitionPermit.description: Required to demolish any building or structure

buildingPermit.description: Required for new construction, additions, and major renovations
stateRequirements: Sealed architectural/structural plans from MA-licensed professional
Energy code compliance (IECC + potentially Stretch Code)

tradePermits.description: Separate permits required for each trade — cannot be bundled in Massachusetts
tradePermits.keyRule: Each trade permit must be submitted by a licensed contractor in that specific trade
